summary.context: Two Flask API endpoints for searching and deleting users, both using SQLite with string concatenation in queries.

findings.title: SQL injection in search_users via string concatenation
findings.issue: search_term from request.args is concatenated directly into SQL query string, allowing arbitrary SQL injection
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with SQLite's ? placeholder
findings.fix_diff:
- query = "SELECT id, username, email FROM users WHERE username LIKE '%" + search_term + "%'"
- cursor.execute(query)
+ cursor.execute("SELECT id, username, email FROM users WHERE username LIKE ?", ('%' + search_term + '%',))

findings.title: SQL injection in delete_user via string concatenation
findings.issue: user_id path parameter is concatenated directly into DELETE query, allowing SQL injection via URL
findings.fix_diff:
- cursor.execute("DELETE FROM users WHERE id = " + user_id)
+ cursor.execute("DELETE FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

findings.title: Missing database connection cleanup (resource leak)
findings.issue: Database connections opened but never closed, leaking file descriptors and database locks
findings.suggestion: Use context manager (with statement) for automatic cleanup
findings.fix_diff:
- conn = sqlite3.connect('app.db')
+ with sqlite3.connect('app.db') as conn:

findings.title: Missing authorization check on delete endpoint
findings.issue: DELETE endpoint has no authentication or authorization, allowing any request to delete any user
findings.suggestion: Add authentication middleware and role-based access control

findings.title: Missing type hints and docstrings
findings.issue: Functions lack type annotations and docstrings
findings.suggestion: Add type hints and brief docstrings for API contract clarity
